narratives.why_changed: Demand Environment declined ~3 points as tier-2 macro inputs deteriorated: consumer sentiment cratering and elevated broad unemployment are now pulling harder against the binding floor. Adoption Momentum remained stable at 76.6 (QoQ acceleration +0.11%, revenue YoY +34%), but the Google Trends regime flipped to 'fading' (−15.4% 4w acceleration), confirming a structural deceleration in mindshare despite strong near-term revenue growth.
narratives.why_not_to_sell: Institutional Confidence anchors the thesis at 94.2 via price momentum (97th pct) and mixed but material 13F holdings (BlackRock $29.7B, State Street $15.2B), yet insider regime is 'heavy_selling' with −$12.4M net, conviction −1.0, offering no buy-side offset. The binding Demand pillar (46.9) reflects genuine macro stress—CPI tilt +0.26, real 10y yield tilt +0.36, VIX contribution +0.062—not measurement noise; until consumer sentiment stabilizes above 55 and unemployment subsides, sector rotation risk remains.
narratives.what_would_break: A drop to 60 (Monitor floor) would require either Institutional Confidence to fall below 84 or Demand Environment to sink below 38; insider cluster buying or CEO accumulation could arrest the slide, but current regime shows no such signal. Structural Review (sub-50) would trigger if both momentum inverts (below 85) and Demand stays below 45 for two consecutive scoring periods, which would force a reassessment of AMD's secular thesis independent of current macro cyclicality.
